Feb 19 · Thursday · Day 1

Arrival & soft landing

~50°F / 10°CEasy dayBeat jet lag
1
AfternoonLand & clear customs

Arrive in Tokyo

Touch down, clear immigration, grab cash and a Suica/Pasmo card, pick up Wi-Fi/SIM. From Narita: N'EX (Tokyo/Shinjuku) or Keisei Skyliner (Ueno). Go slow.

Get SuicaNarita (NRT)~60 min N'EX
Open in Maps
🚂N'EX/Skyliner into Tokyo (~60 min, ~¥3,000); final leg set with hotel
2
Late afternoon~4:00 PM

Check in & drop bagsEasy day

Check in, drop bags, freshen up. Resist the nap - short reset only, stay up to beat jet lag.

Hotel TBDShort rest

🚂Gentle stroll near hotel
3
Evening5:30-7:00 PM

Gentle stroll

Relaxed walk to shake off the flight - neon, side streets, vending machines. No agenda.

Easy walkFirst neon

🚂To nearest convenience store
4
Night7:30 PM

Conbini dinner & sleep

Round one at 7-Eleven / Lawson / FamilyMart: egg sando, onigiri, hot oden. Then sleep early.

~¥1,000 (~$7)Egg sandoEarly night

Feb 20 · Friday · Day 2

West Tokyo: shrines, style & neon

~50°F / 10°C, clearYamanote day1 reservation
1
Morning8:00-11:00 AM

Quiet & sacred - Meiji Shrine

Start at Meiji Shrine at opening: the path is nearly empty, priests sweeping, jet lag fades. 10-min walk to Harajuku.

Free entryOpens ~6:40amMeiji-jingumae
Open in Maps
🚂10-min walk south via Omotesando to Harajuku
2
Late morning11:00 AM-1:00 PM

Harajuku + lunch

Takeshita St for kawaii energy + a crepe, then Cat Street for real streetwear. Your clothes window.

This is where you'll have lunch
🍲 Eat nearbyAfuri - yuzu ramenMaisen - tonkatsuGyoza Lou
Clothes shoppingOmotesando lunch

🚂JR Yamanote: Harajuku → Shibuya, 1 stop / 2 min
3
Early afternoon1:30-4:00 PM

Shibuya + gaming

Scramble Crossing + Shibuya Sky, then Parco 6F: Nintendo, Pokemon, Jump Shop, Capcom. Budget 1.5 hrs.

🍲 Eat nearbyIchiran - tonkotsuStreamer Coffee
Parco 6FShibuya SkyShibuya Stn

🚂JR Yamanote: Shibuya → Takadanobaba, 6 stops / 13 min, then 10-min walk
4
Late afternoon4:30-5:10 PM

Capybaras at CapinessReservation required

5 min north to Takadanobaba for Capiness: 40 min petting & feeding capybaras. Mellow palate-cleanser.

¥4,000 (~$27)Book aheadTakadanobaba

Shinjuku neon & food

Feb 21 · Saturday · Day 3

Historic East: coffee, temples & ceramics

~50°F / 10°CCoffee + craftWalkable east side
1
Morning8:30-11:00 AM

Kuramae coffee & craft

Slow start in Kuramae ('Tokyo's Brooklyn'): specialty roasters, a Sumida riverside walk, and craft shops. Build a notebook at Kakimori, browse maker/ceramics boutiques.

🍲 Eat nearbyLeaves Coffee RoastersCoffee WrightsKogei
Specialty coffeeKakimori notebooksRiver walk
Open in Maps
🚂10-min riverside walk north to Asakusa
2
Late morning11:00 AM-1:00 PM

Senso-ji & Asakusa + lunch

Tokyo's oldest temple: Kaminarimon gate, the pagoda, and Nakamise street snacks. Lunch nearby - tempura or unagi.

This is where you'll have lunch
🍲 Eat nearbyDaikokuya tempuraAsakusa unagi
Free templeNakamise snacksAsakusa Stn

🚂10-min walk to Kappabashi Kitchen Town
3
Early afternoon1:15-3:00 PM

Kappabashi ceramics

Kitchen Town: bowls, plates, sake cups, lacquerware + world-class knives. Ship big buys home via takkyubin.

Bowls & platesKnivesOpen Sat

Evening6:00-9:00 PM

Akihabara electric town

Super Potato retro games, arcades, gachapon halls, neon everywhere. Dinner: ramen or a themed cafe. Your gamer must-do.
